---
title: "Untitled"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-22"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
library(nycflights13)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(lubridate)
library(boot)
```

```{r}
#filtering flights with the important variables we chose
Filtered_flights <- flights[, c("year", "month", "day", "dep_delay", "arr_delay", "dest", "origin", "time_hour")]

Filtered_flights %>% count(year, month, day) %>% filter(n > 1)

summary(Filtered_flights)
#view(Filtered_flights)


#filtering weather with the important variables we chose
filtered_weather <- weather[,c(1,6,8,12,14,15)]

summary(filtered_weather)
#view(filtered_weather)

#combining both filtered datasets by time_hour and origin
cleaned_weather_flights <- inner_join(Filtered_flights, filtered_weather, by = c("origin", "time_hour"))
view(cleaned_weather_flights)
cleaned_weather_flights
cleaned_weather_flights <- drop_na(cleaned_weather_flights)

```




**Q.1** How do holidays/popular vacation times affect delays?

```{r}
month_delays <- cleaned_weather_flights |>
  group_by(month) |>
  summarise(avg_arr = mean(arr_delay),
            avg_dep = mean(dep_delay),
            total = n(),
            total_arr_delayed = sum(arr_delay > 15),
            total_dep_delayed = sum(dep_delay > 15),
            percent_arr_delay = total_arr_delayed/total,
            percent_dep_delay = total_dep_delayed/total) |>
  arrange(month) |>
  mutate(abbre_month = c("Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun", "Jul", "Aug", "Sept", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"))

# Months w/ most flights
month_delays |> arrange(desc(total))

# Months with the most departure delays on average
month_delays |> arrange(desc(avg_dep))

# Months w/ most arrival delays on average
month_delays |> arrange(desc(avg_arr))

# Months w/ high % of departure delays
month_delays |> arrange(desc(percent_dep_delay))

# Months w/ high % of arrival delays
month_delays |> arrange(desc(percent_arr_delay))

# Top 3 months w/ highest delays: July, June, December
# Months with the most amount of flights: August, October, July
# Flights mostly within the 26k - 28.5k range except for February with 23k flights
```

```{r}
library(gridExtra)
barplot1 <- ggplot(month_delays, aes(x = reorder(abbre_month, -month))) +
  geom_col(aes(y = avg_dep, fill = avg_dep < 0), position = "dodge") +
  labs(x = "Month", 
       y = "Average Flight Delay (mins)",
       title = "Average Departure Delay by Month") +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  coord_flip()

barplot2 <- ggplot(month_delays, aes(x = reorder(abbre_month, -month))) +
  geom_col(aes(y = avg_arr, fill = avg_arr < 0), position = "dodge") +
  labs(x = "Month", 
       y = "Average Flight Delay (mins)",
       title = "Average Arrival Delay by Month") +
  coord_flip()

grid.arrange(barplot1, barplot2, ncol = 2)
```

```{r}
per_bar <- ggplot(month_delays, aes(x = reorder(abbre_month, -month))) +
  geom_col(aes(y = percent_dep_delay, fill = percent_dep_delay > 0.27), position = "dodge") +
  labs(x = "Month", 
       y = "% of Flights Delayed",
       title = "% of Depart Delayed Flights by Month") +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  coord_flip()

per_bar2 <- ggplot(month_delays, aes(x = reorder(abbre_month, -month))) +
  geom_col(aes(y = percent_arr_delay, fill = percent_arr_delay > 0.28), position = "dodge") +
  labs(x = "Month", 
       y = "% of Flights Delayed",
       title = "% of Arrival Delayed Flights by Month") +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  coord_flip()

grid.arrange(per_bar, per_bar2, ncol = 2)
```



**Q.2** Do popular vacation locations mean an increase in flight delays?

```{r}
dest_delay <- cleaned_weather_flights |>
  group_by(dest) |>
  summarise(total_flights = n(),
            total_arr_delayed = sum(arr_delay > 15),
            total_dep_delayed = sum(dep_delay > 15),
            percent_arr_delay = total_arr_delayed/total_flights,
            percent_dep_delay = total_dep_delayed/total_flights)
  
dest_delay |> arrange(desc(total_flights))
# lots of flights going into Atlanta (GA), Chicago (IL) and Los Angeles (LAX) in 2013

dest_delay |> arrange(desc(percent_arr_delay))
# CAE, OKC, TUL

dest_delay |> arrange(desc(percent_dep_delay))
# JAC, SBN, CAE, TUL, OKC
# Keep in mind JAC and SBN both have less than 25 flights

# CAE: 63% arrival delay and 48% departure delay
# OKC: 50% arrival delay and 43% departure delay
# TUL: 48% arrival delay and 44% departure delay

# Popular airport spots yet they seem to run pretty efficiently
# ATL: 25% arrival delay and 20% departure delay
# ORD: 23% arrival delay and 22% departure delay
# LAX: 20% arrival delay and 17% departure delay

```

```{r}
# Finding destinations with the most % of flights delayed (departure + arrival)

top5_arr_delayed <- dest_delay |>
  arrange(desc(percent_arr_delay)) |>
  slice(1:5)

top5_dep_delayed <- dest_delay |>
  arrange(desc(percent_dep_delay)) |>
  slice(1:5)

b1 <- ggplot(top5_arr_delayed, mapping = aes(x = reorder(dest, -percent_arr_delay))) +
  geom_col(aes(y = percent_arr_delay), fill = "salmon", position = "dodge") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(x = "Destination", 
       y = "% of Flights Delayed",
       title = "Top 5 Destinations w/ Arrival Delays") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

b2 <- ggplot(top5_dep_delayed, mapping = aes(x = reorder(dest, -percent_dep_delay))) +
  geom_col(aes(y = percent_dep_delay), fill = "salmon", position = "dodge") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(x = "Destination",
       y = "% of Flights Delayed",
       title = "Top 5 Destinations w/ Departure Delays") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
  

grid.arrange(b1, b2, ncol = 2)
```


```{r}
# Comparing with delays from busiest locations

busiest_dest_delay <- dest_delay |>
  arrange(desc(total_flights)) |>
  slice(1:5)

g1 <- ggplot(busiest_dest_delay, mapping = aes(x = reorder(dest, -percent_arr_delay))) +
  geom_col(aes(y = percent_arr_delay, fill = dest), position = "dodge") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(x = "Destination", 
       y = "% of Flights Delayed",
       title = "Arrival Delays in Top 5 Destinations") 

g2 <- ggplot(busiest_dest_delay, mapping = aes(x = reorder(dest, -percent_dep_delay))) +
  geom_col(aes(y = percent_dep_delay, fill = dest), position = "dodge") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(x = "Destination", 
       y = "% of Flights Delayed",
       title = "Departure Delays in Top 5 Destinations")

grid.arrange(g1, g2, ncol = 2)

```



**Q.3** Do specific weather conditions and seasons delay flights more than others?
```{r}
#Checking percentage of flights by Seasons
#grouping months into seasons
flights_season <- cleaned_weather_flights %>%
  mutate(season = case_when(
    month %in% c(12, 1, 2) ~ "Winter",
    month %in% c(3, 4, 5) ~ "Spring",
    month %in% c(6, 7, 8) ~ "Summer",
    month %in% c(9, 10, 11) ~ "Fall"
  ))

flights_season

Percent_delay_by_Seasons <- flights_season %>% 
  group_by(season) %>%
  summarise(total_flights = n(),
            total_arr_delayed = sum(arr_delay > 15),
            total_dep_delayed = sum(dep_delay > 15),
            percent_arr_delay = total_arr_delayed/total_flights,
            percent_dep_delay = total_dep_delayed/total_flights)

Percent_delay_by_Seasons


#Spring: 24.2% Arrival Delay and 22.4% Departure Delay
#Summer: 28.6% Arrival Delay and 26.6% Departure Delay
#Winter: 26.2% Arrival Delay and 22.2% Departure Delay
#Fall: 15.7% Arrival Delay and 14.7% Departure Delay
```
#---------------------------looking at the linear model-----------------------------------



```{r,echo=FALSE}
MLR_season_arr_delay <-  lm(arr_delay ~ season, flights_season)
summary(MLR_season_arr_delay)
```

Based on the on the linear regression model, the seasons are a major factor as all have a p-value < 0.05
Fall: avgs -1 min delays
Spring: avgs 8 min delay
Summer: avgs 14 min delay
Winter: avgs 10 min delay
The fall season may be the best to fly at.

```{r,echo=FALSE}
MLR_season_dep_delay <-  lm(dep_delay ~ season, flights_season)
summary(MLR_season_dep_delay)
```

When looking at the linear model of the rain & visibility on the average mines of arrival delays it seems like rain and visibility has an effect to flight delays as the p-value <0.05, however they may not be significant as they both only explain 2% of the data

Also let it be know that in the bin[0.9,1.2) has a p-value >0.05, meaning that this area is not statistically significant, meaning there may not be enough evidence to conclude the association with delays in that category

For visibility, for every 1-mile increase in visibility, delays decrease by 2 minutes.
For humidity it is statistically significant however its effects are small compared to the other factors.

#------------------------------------------Weather Conditions------------------------------------------
```{r,echo=FALSE}
#Checking percentage of flights by rain 

flights_rain

#finding percentage of delays per 0.3 inches of rain
Avg_delay_per_rain <- flights_rain %>% 
  group_by(precip_bin) %>% 
  summarise(total_flights = n(),
            total_arr_delayed = sum(arr_delay > 15),
            total_dep_delayed = sum(dep_delay > 15),
            percent_arr_delay = total_arr_delayed/total_flights,
            percent_dep_delay = total_dep_delayed/total_flights)

Avg_delay_per_rain

#0.0 - 0.3 = barely rain: 23.6% Arrival Delay and 21.3% Departure Delay
#0.3 - 0.6 = little rain: 60.5% Arrival Delay and 50.5% Departure Delay
#0.6 - 0.9 = regular rain: 73.9% Arrival Delay and 45.6% Departure Delay
#0.9 - 1.2 = heavy rain: 45% Arrival Delay and 50% Departure Delay
#1.2 above = very heavy rain: 66.6% Arrival Delay and 88.8% Departure Delay

#Note: it seems like although there is a relationship with higher rain and delays, in the heavy rain category it has less delays than the rest


Graph_1 <- ggplot(Avg_delay_per_rain, aes(x = precip_bin)) +
  geom_col(aes(y = percent_arr_delay, fill = "Arrival Delay"), position = position_dodge()) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(x = "Precipitation Bin (inches)", y = "Percent Delayed", fill = "Delay Type",
       title = "Flight Delays vs Precipitation") +
  coord_flip()

Graph_2 <- ggplot(Avg_delay_per_rain, aes(x = precip_bin)) +
  geom_col(aes(y = percent_dep_delay, fill = "Departure Delay"), position = position_dodge()) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(x = "Precipitation Bin (inches)", y = "Percent Delayed", fill = "Delay Type",
       title = "Flight Delays vs Precipitation") +
  coord_flip()
  
grid.arrange(Graph_1, Graph_2, ncol = 2)
```
0.0 - 0.3 = barely rain: 23.6% Arrival Delay and 21.3% Departure Delay
0.3 - 0.6 = little rain: 60.5% Arrival Delay and 50.5% Departure Delay
0.6 - 0.9 = regular rain: 73.9% Arrival Delay and 45.6% Departure Delay
0.9 - 1.2 = heavy rain: 45% Arrival Delay and 50% Departure Delay
1.2 above = very heavy rain: 66.6% Arrival Delay and 88.8% Departure Delay

Note: it seems like although there is a relationship with higher rain and delays, in the heavy rain category it has less delays than the rest



```{r,echo=FALSE}
#By visibility

#finding percentage of delays per visibility
Avg_delay_per_vis <- cleaned_weather_flights %>% 
  group_by(visib) %>% 
  summarise(total_flights = n(),
            total_arr_delayed = sum(arr_delay > 15),
            total_dep_delayed = sum(dep_delay > 15),
            percent_arr_delay = total_arr_delayed/total_flights,
            percent_dep_delay = total_dep_delayed/total_flights)

Avg_delay_per_vis 


Graph1 <- ggplot(Avg_delay_per_vis , mapping = aes(x = visib)) +
  geom_col(aes(y = percent_arr_delay, fill = "Arrival Delay"), position = "dodge") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(y = "Percent of Flights Delayed", fill = "Delay Type",
       title = "Flight Delays by Visibility") 

Graph2 <- ggplot(Avg_delay_per_vis , mapping = aes(x = visib)) +
  geom_col(aes(y = percent_dep_delay, fill = "Departure Delay"), position = "dodge") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(y = "Percent of Flights Delayed", fill = "Delay Type",
       title = "Flight Delays by Visibility") 

grid.arrange(Graph1, Graph2, ncol = 2)
#Note: based on the graph there is a correlation to delays and visibility however at the very low visibility there are some variables that are not high, probably due to not enough data/outliers
```
Note: based on the graph there is a correlation to delays and visibility however at the very low visibility there are some variables that are not high, probably due to not enough data/outliers


```{r,echo=FALSE}
#By Temp
flights_temp <- cleaned_weather_flights %>%
  mutate(temp_group = case_when(
    temp < 40            ~ "Low",
    temp >= 40 & temp < 70 ~ "Medium",
    temp >= 70           ~ "High",
  ))
flights_temp

Avg_delay_per_temp <- flights_temp %>% 
  group_by(temp_group) %>% 
  summarise(total_flights = n(),
            total_arr_delayed = sum(arr_delay > 15),
            total_dep_delayed = sum(dep_delay > 15),
            percent_arr_delay = total_arr_delayed/total_flights,
            percent_dep_delay = total_dep_delayed/total_flights)

Avg_delay_per_temp

#Based on the data there might not be a major correlation to delays
```

Based on the data there might not be a major correlation to delays


```{r,echo=FALSE}
seasons_avg_temp <- flights_season %>% group_by(season) %>% summarise(
  avg_temp = mean(temp)
)

seasons_avg_temp
```
Temperature can be grouped with season, as the delays for seasons & temp are similar to each other. Temperature is strongly correlated with seasons, so including both in the same model may create redundancy.


```{r,echo=FALSE}
#By humidity
flights_hum <- cleaned_weather_flights %>%
  mutate(humidity_group = case_when(
    humid < 30             ~ "Low",
    humid >= 30 & humid < 60 ~ "Medium",
    humid >= 60             ~ "High",
  ))
flights_hum

Avg_delay_per_humid <- flights_hum %>% 
  group_by(humidity_group) %>% 
  summarise(total_flights = n(),
            total_arr_delayed = sum(arr_delay > 15),
            total_dep_delayed = sum(dep_delay > 15),
            percent_arr_delay = total_arr_delayed/total_flights,
            percent_dep_delay = total_dep_delayed/total_flights)

Avg_delay_per_humid

#Based on the data there also might be a low correlation with humidity and delays
```
Based on the data there also might be a low correlation with humidity and delays


#------------------Linear Model------------------------------------

```{r, echo=FALSE,echo=FALSE}
weather_lm_arr <- lm(arr_delay ~ precip_bin + visib + humid ,flights_rain)

summary(weather_lm_arr)
```
When looking at the linear model of the rain & visibility on the average mines of arrival delays it seems like rain and visibility has an effect to flight delays as the p-value <0.05, however they may not be significant as they both only explain 2% of the data

Also let it be know that in the bin[0.9,1.2) has a p-value >0.05, meaning that this area is not statistically significant, meaning there may not be enough evidence to conclude the association with delays in that category

For visibility it seems like for every mile visibility increase the deplays decrease by 2 minutes
For humidity it is statistically significant however its effects are small compared to the other factors

```{r,echo=FALSE}
weather_lm_dep <- lm(dep_delay ~ precip_bin + visib + humid ,flights_rain)

summary(weather_lm_dep)
```

When looking at the linear model of the rain & visibility on the average mina of dep delays it seems like rain and visibility has an effect to flight delays as the p-value <0.05, however they may not be significant as they both only explain 1% of the data

Once again, the bin[0.9,1.2) has a p-value >0.05, meaning that this area is not statistically significant, meaning there may not be enough evidence to conclude the association with delays in that category

For visibility it seems like for every mile visibility increases the delays decrease by 1.8 minutes
For humidity it is statistically significant however its effects are small compared to the other factors

```{r,echo=FALSE}
Percent_delay_by_Seasons %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(percent_arr_delay, percent_dep_delay),
               names_to = "delay_type",
               values_to = "percent_delay") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = season, y = percent_delay * 100, fill = delay_type)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  labs(
    title = "Flight Delay Percentage by Season",
    x = "Season",
    y = "Proportion of Flights Delayed (>15 minutes)",
    fill = "Delay Type"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("percent_arr_delay" = "steelblue",
                               "percent_dep_delay" = "tomato"),
                    labels = c("Arrival Delay", "Departure Delay")) +
  theme_minimal()

scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent)
```



*Do specific weather conditions and seasons delay flights more than others?*
Both seasons and weathers have statistically significant effects on flight delays. Flights in the summer are way worse than flights in fall, which seems to be like the best time to fly.When taking a look at the weather condition, we can see that the temperature strongly correlates to the season and the rain and visibility shows. We can see that the more rain there is the higher delays in minutes and when the visibility increases each mile the delay also decrease around every two minutes. Humidity is also significant but the affects to delays are very small. So weather and seasons may be a cause in delays but their effects are not very big as they only explain a small variation of the data with them explanation about 1-2% of the data, meaning there may be way more factor correlating with these variables in determining delays.




**Q.4** Do flight delays occur due to their destination or the origin of the airport?
```{r, echo=FALSE}

#Checking the Average arrival delay of destinations with top 20 airports
top_dest <- flights %>%
  group_by(dest) %>%
  summarize(avg_arr_delay = mean(arr_delay, na.rm = TRUE),
            avg_dep_delay = mean(dep_delay, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  slice_max(avg_arr_delay, n = 20)

bar_1 <- ggplot(top_dest, aes(x = reorder(dest, avg_arr_delay), y = avg_arr_delay, fill = dest)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = "Destination", y = "Average Arrival Delay (min)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  guides(fill = "none")

#compare the departure delay with the destinations with the highest arrival delay
bar_2 <- ggplot(top_dest, aes(x = reorder(dest, avg_arr_delay), y = avg_dep_delay, fill = dest)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = "Destination", y = "Average Departure Delay (min)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  guides(fill = "none")

grid.arrange(bar_1,bar_2, ncol = 2)
```


```{r}
dest_lm_arr <- lm(arr_delay ~ dest ,cleaned_weather_flights)

summary(dest_lm_arr)
```



```{r, echo=FALSE}

#Checking the Average arrival delay of origin
Avg_Origin_delays <- cleaned_weather_flights %>% 
  group_by(origin) %>% 
  summarise(total_flights = n(),
            total_arr_delayed = sum(arr_delay > 15),
            total_dep_delayed = sum(dep_delay > 15),
            percent_arr_delay = total_arr_delayed/total_flights,
            percent_dep_delay = total_dep_delayed/total_flights)
  
  
Avg_Origin_delays
#The 3 airports in New York seems to run efficiently
#EWR: 25.5% Arrival Delay and 24.5% Departure Delay
#JFK: 22.9% Arrival Delay and 20% Departure Delay
#LGA: 22.3% Arrival Delay and 18.8% Departure Delay


#compared to the nation average
#using data from destinations to find national average
Nation_Average_delays <- percent_delay %>% 
  summarise(total_flights = n(),
             mean_avg_arr = mean(percent_arr_delay),
      mean_avg_dep = mean(percent_dep_delay))
    
 Nation_Average_delays   

#National average: 26.1% Arrival Delay and 25% Departure Delay   
 

```


**Q.5**Are flight delays more likely during the holidays?
```{r}
# Defining holday dates for year 2013
holiday_dates <- cleaned_weather_flights %>%
  mutate(
    date = as.Date(time_hour),
    holiday_period = case_when(
      date %in% seq(as.Date("2013-01-01"), as.Date("2013-01-01"), by = "day") ~ "New Year's",
      date %in% seq(as.Date("2013-05-25"), as.Date("2013-05-27"), by = "day") ~ "Memorial Day",
      date %in% seq(as.Date("2013-07-04"), as.Date("2013-07-07"), by = "day") ~ "Independence Day",
      date %in% seq(as.Date("2013-11-28"), as.Date("2013-12-01"), by = "day") ~ "Thanksgiving",
      date %in% seq(as.Date("2013-12-24"), as.Date("2013-12-31"), by = "day") ~ "Christmas/NYE",
      TRUE ~ "Non-Holiday"
    )
  )

# Summarizing delays for holidays
holiday_delays <- holiday_dates %>%
  group_by(holiday_period) %>%
  summarise(
    total_flights = n(),
    total_arr_delayed = sum(arr_delay > 15),
    total_dep_delayed = sum(dep_delay > 15),
    percent_arr_delay = total_arr_delayed / total_flights,
    percent_dep_delay = total_dep_delayed / total_flights
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(percent_dep_delay))

holiday_delays
```
```{r}
# Comparing percentage of delays for each holiday and non-holidays on average
h1 <- ggplot(holiday_delays, aes(x = reorder(holiday_period, -percent_arr_delay))) +
  geom_col(aes(y = percent_arr_delay, fill = holiday_period == "Non-Holiday"), position = "dodge") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(x = "Period", y = "% of Flights Delayed",
       title = "Holiday vs Non-Holiday:\n Arrival Delays") +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  coord_flip()


h2 <- ggplot(holiday_delays, aes(x = reorder(holiday_period, -percent_dep_delay))) +
  geom_col(aes(y = percent_dep_delay, fill = holiday_period == "Non-Holiday"), position = "dodge") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(x = "Period", y = "% of Flights Delayed",
       title = "Holiday vs Non-Holiday:\n Departure Delays") +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  coord_flip()

grid.arrange(h1, h2, ncol = 2)
```
The 3 airports in New York seems to run efficiently
EWR: 25.5% Arrival Delay and 24.5% Departure Delay
JFK: 22.9% Arrival Delay and 20% Departure Delay
LGA: 22.3% Arrival Delay and 18.8% Departure Delay

National average: 26.1% Arrival Delay and 25% Departure Delay   
 
```{r, echo = false}
origin_lm_dep <- lm(dep_delay ~ origin ,cleaned_weather_flights)

summary(origin_lm_dep)
```

```{r, echo=FALSE}
origin_lm_arr <- lm(arr_delay ~ origin ,cleaned_weather_flights)

summary(origin_lm_arr)
``` 
**Do flight delays occur due to their destination or the origin of the airport?**
Destinations:
Based on the linear model, although there are some destinations that are not statistically significant, destinations do play a role in flight delays. However, when looking at the r^2, we can see it only explains 1% of the data, showing that destinations are not a major factor when determining flight delays alone.

Origins:
For the origin based on the linear model, it shows there is a relationship with each of the three origin flights in NYC, as they are all statistically significant with a p-value less than 0.05. EWR leading to 9 minutes of delays, JFK and LFA arrive 3 minutes early. However, the origins only explain less that 1% of the delays, meaning they are not a major factor in explaining the data alone.
```{r}
holiday_long <- holiday_delays %>%
  select(holiday_period, percent_arr_delay, percent_dep_delay) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(percent_arr_delay, percent_dep_delay),
               names_to = "delay_type",
               values_to = "percent") %>%
  mutate(delay_type = recode(delay_type,
                             "percent_arr_delay" = "Arrival Delay",
                             "percent_dep_delay" = "Departure Delay"))

# Combined plot
ggplot(holiday_long, aes(x = holiday_period, y = percent, fill = delay_type)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(x = "Period",
       y = "% of Flights Delayed",
       fill = "Delay Type",
       title = "Flight Delays: Holiday vs Non-Holiday") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 10))
```

```{r}
holiday_dates_summary <- cleaned_weather_flights %>%
  mutate(
    date = as.Date(time_hour),
    holiday_period = case_when(
      date %in% seq(as.Date("2013-01-01"), as.Date("2013-01-01"), by = "day") |
      date %in% seq(as.Date("2013-05-25"), as.Date("2013-05-27"), by = "day") |
      date %in% seq(as.Date("2013-07-04"), as.Date("2013-07-07"), by = "day")|
      date %in% seq(as.Date("2013-11-28"), as.Date("2013-12-01"), by = "day") |
      date %in% seq(as.Date("2013-12-24"), as.Date("2013-12-31"), by = "day") ~ "Holiday",
      TRUE ~ "Non-Holiday"
    )
  )

holiday_delays <- holiday_dates_summary %>%
  group_by(holiday_period) %>%
  summarise(
    total_flights = n(),
    total_arr_delayed = sum(arr_delay > 15),
    total_dep_delayed = sum(dep_delay > 15),
    percent_arr_delay = total_arr_delayed / total_flights,
    percent_dep_delay = total_dep_delayed / total_flights
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(percent_dep_delay))

holiday_long <- holiday_delays %>%
  select(holiday_period, percent_arr_delay, percent_dep_delay) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(percent_arr_delay, percent_dep_delay),
               names_to = "delay_type",
               values_to = "percent") %>%
  mutate(delay_type = recode(delay_type,
                             "percent_arr_delay" = "Arrival Delay",
                             "percent_dep_delay" = "Departure Delay"))

# Combined plot
ggplot(holiday_long, aes(x = holiday_period, y = percent, fill = delay_type)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(x = "Period",
       y = "% of Flights Delayed",
       fill = "Delay Type",
       title = "Flight Delays: Holiday vs Non-Holiday") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 10))
